p.seg: To all Christ's faithful who shall view or hear this writing, Walter son of Alan, steward of the lord king, sends greeting in the Lord.

to the honour of God, and for the souls of my lords the kings of Scotland, and for my own soul, and the soul of my wife and of all my ancestors and heirs, to have given, granted, and by this my present charter to have made firm, to God and the monastery at Melrose, and to the monks in that place serving God now and for ever in the future, the territory of Mauchline, and the church of St Michael to be found there. I have indeed granted to them, and have made firm by this my charter, the whole territory, mentioned before, by its own boundaries, that is, as they are contained in my charter which the monks have concerning it, so that they should have everything contained within the boundaries already said, with all liberties and pertinents that belong to the same holding by right or long-standing custom. I and my heirs shall warrant and defend the said monks against all men, and make them possess in peace all the things said above, with their pertinents. To be held and had entirely free and quit from every earthly service and every custom and exaction and demand. In addition, the five merks which the aforesaid monks were bound to have to pay to me and and my heirs in return for the same land each year according to the words of my charter and the charter of my father, to the same monks, in the spirit affection and charity, I have, for my part and for all my heirs and successors, perpetually remitted and made them free and immune for ever from the payment of them. I have also freed and entirely released the same land and its pertinents from hunting and the capture of birds which I and my ancestors were accustomed to excercise in the free land of the said church of Mauchline and from the practice of any art and business whatever.
And so that my donation and confirmation should be made firm and become lasting, without any impediment, I have strengthened the present writing by the application of my seal.

With these witnesses: the lord John of Maxwell, Berhard Fraser, Adam son of Gilbert, Reginald of Crawford, Roger son of Glay, Robert Logan, Robert of Crawford, Nigel de Heriz, Robert the clerk, and many others.
